parsed_review.summary: CP00-318 is a Risk-C, 150-unit descriptor-only CRM-core (RP09) closeout pack that closes the RP09.P07 failure phase (M09 Claude-review failure tail, M10 closeout head) and opens the RP09.P08 Hermes evidence foundation (M00 scope inventory through M08 head). Full read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is supported and not overstated. Verified: (1) pack-binding chain is continuous — CP317.next_pack_id=CP00-318, CP317.next_subphase=RP09.P07.M09.S03=CP318.first_unit_id; CP318.next_pack_id=CP00-319, next_subphase=RP09.P08.M08.S02 — and is identical across registry.js, contract JSON current_pack, manifest, and baseline. (2) Counts are internally consistent and match the prompt: 150 units; deliverable_counts sum to 150 (failure_recovery 20, security_audit 3, implementation 41, hermes_evidence 73, fixture 1, test 7, claude_review 5); phase_counts P07:29/P08:121=150; micro_title_row_counts=150; spot-checked security_audit=3 and claude_review=5 against baseline included_units. (3) All 7 declared public exports exist (2 in service.js, 5 in validators.js) and are re-exported via index.js. (4) No-write/no-real-data boundaries are strong: freezeCp318Result sets every runtime/write/storage/leak flag false with synthetic_only/no_real_data true, and inherited CP317 row-extras provide cross-tenant deny, no lock acquisition, rollback descriptor-only, and blocked-claim/Hermes-body exclusion that the descriptor validator enforces. (5) Authority boundary is correct — claude_is_final_approval false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening true, local validation does not claim enterprise trust; the Claude review packet is read-only with allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob only. (6) Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor receipt/packet shapes (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_..._no_write), not product runtime. (7) No overclaim: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both declare production_ready:false / implemented_pending_review, with internal production_ready_candidate:true correctly labeled a candidate pending human-approved final closeout. (8) CP178+ review hardening present: forbidden_review_evidence rejects auth failures, zero-byte/malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing source-inspection/raw-output references, and final-approval/enterprise-trust claims. (9) Scope hygiene is correct — the 7 modified tracked files plus the new manifest dir are in scope, while unrelated dirty files (.docx, LDIP c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json, workbook/) are preserved as out-of-scope and not inferred into this pack. Tests (3 CP318 cases) and the validator assert coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, M03 guard flags, authority boundary, and CP00-318→CP00-319 handoff routing. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; this review does not block pack or goal closeout (human final approval still required before any runtime opening).
